directive.title: RFQ list API endpoint with Neon DB query layer

auditor.summary: The implementation successfully delivers both endpoints per specification. GET /api/rfq implements proper pagination with limit/offset, parameter clamping, parallel query execution via Promise.all, structured logging, and error handling without stack trace leakage. GET /api/health/db correctly measures latency using performance.now(), returns proper success/failure responses, and logs structured errors. All imports resolve to allowed packages or local paths. No console.log/error usage. Only minor issues found: unnecessary type assertion and unused type alias in rfq route, and a defensive error handling improvement in health route. The build is production-ready and meets all acceptance criteria.

top_issues.description: Type assertion 'data as RFQRecord[]' on line 69 is unnecessary since db.select() already returns the correct type from Drizzle inference. While not harmful, it's redundant and could mask future type mismatches.
The RFQRecord type alias on line 7 is defined but only used in the type assertion on line 69. If the type assertion is removed, the type alias becomes unused.

The error variable on line 31 is cast as Error but could be any type thrown. While this is standard practice, a more robust approach would check if err is actually an Error instance before accessing .message and .stack.
